findings.description: Merged cells in rows 11/14/2013 and 9/11/2014 were split, causing content duplication across columns
findings.evidence: PDF: '11/14/2013' and '9/11/2014' rows have text spanning across 'Jordan Lake Water Supply Allocations' and 'Cape Fear River Basin Water Supply Plan' columns as a single merged cell. HTML: The text is duplicated independently in both columns rather than merged.

findings.description: Merged cell 'EMC Model Approval' in the Cape Fear – Neuse River Basin Hydrologic Model column spans multiple rows (approximately 1/1/2013 through 5/1/2013) in the PDF but is duplicated as separate identical cell values in four rows (1/1/2013, 2/1/2013, 4/1/2013, 5/1/2013) in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a single merged cell with 'EMC Model Approval' spanning rows. HTML repeats 'EMC Model Approval' in four separate rows.

findings.description: The 11/14/2013 row in the PDF has a single merged cell spanning the 'Jordan Lake Water Supply Allocations' and 'Cape Fear River Basin Water Supply Plan' columns. The HTML duplicates the text into both columns as separate cells.
findings.evidence: PDF: single merged cell reads 'Final Water Supply Plan and DWR's Allocation Recommendations presented at the EMC's November 2013 meeting.' spanning columns 2–3. HTML: identical text appears in two separate <td> elements.

findings.description: The 9/11/2014 row in the PDF has a single merged cell spanning the 'Jordan Lake Water Supply Allocations' and 'Cape Fear River Basin Water Supply Plan' columns. The HTML duplicates the text into both columns.
findings.evidence: PDF: single merged cell reads 'EMC decision at the September 2014 meeting.' spanning columns 2–3. HTML: identical text in two separate <td> elements.
